decisions.reasoning: Q3 FY26 beat consensus (EPS $4.27 vs $4.07 est, Azure +40% YoY, AI ARR $37B at +123% YoY). Class action lawsuit covers class period May 2025-Jan 2026, backward-looking legal overhang not new fundamental deterioration. Bear risks real: Copilot NPS -19.8, only 4% M365 seat penetration after 18 months, $37.5B/qtr capex squeezing FCF. Stock shed 8.59% last week, both SMAs declining. Holding: fundamentals improved in Q3, 54 analysts avg target $528.88, selling at -12% into sentiment-driven weakness is poor timing. Monitor Q4 FY26 (late July) as next thesis test.

sources.headline: Microsoft (MSFT) Faces Class-Action Lawsuit Over Azure Growth Concerns
Q3 FY26 EPS $4.27 vs $4.07 est, Azure +40%, AI ARR $37B at +123% YoY
Microsoft bear case: Copilot NPS -19.8, 20M seats (4% M365 penetration), capex concerns

decisions.invalidation: Q4 FY26 Azure growth decelerates below 35%, or Copilot paid seat count does not improve materially from 20M

decisions.reasoning: B200 GPU rental prices fell 31% from peak $6.11 to $4.22 by June 21 (CNBC/Kalshi, June 22) - real pricing pressure. However SpaceX/Google $920M/month deal using 110k NVDA GPUs validates physical AI demand. Annual shareholder meeting June 24 is near-term catalyst for Blackwell/Vera ramp commentary. RBC Capital bullish for H2 2026 and 2027. 62 analysts avg Strong Buy, target $298.93. YTD +12% massively lags SMH +85% - rotation risk into memory/AI tools. Hold through shareholder meeting before reassessing.

sources.headline: Nvidia's stock struggles as Kalshi traders bet chip prices are coming down
Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Stumbles as Kalshi Traders Turn Bearish on Chip Prices
NVIDIA 2026 Shareholder Meeting: Blackwell and Vera production ramp focus; SpaceX/Google GPU deal $920M/month

decisions.invalidation: B200 rental prices fall below $3.50, or shareholder meeting June 24 reveals Blackwell/Vera ramp disappointment, or NVDA misses revenue guidance

decisions.reasoning: Only position with a long SMA signal (fast SMA $723.51 > slow SMA $722.64). Broad tech exposure benefits from AI capex supercycle. Goldman raised S&P 500 target to 8000, hyperscaler capex approaching $800B in 2026. Macro tailwind intact. No catalyst to exit.

sources.headline: S&P 500 forecast to climb as earnings growth powers stocks higher; target raised to 8000
2026 Mid-Year Outlook: U.S. Stocks and Economy positive

decisions.invalidation: QQQ fast SMA crosses below slow SMA (signal flips flat/short), or inflation re-accelerates forcing Fed hawkishness

decisions.reasoning: Only green position (+0.65%). Portfolio ballast at S&P ATH. Macro supportive - seven consecutive bullish sessions, reduced Middle East tensions, declining bond yields. SMA signal is flat but macro momentum positive. Thin equity risk premium and stretched positioning are reasons not to add, but no reason to exit this small position.

sources.headline: 2026 Mid-Year Outlook positive; S&P 500 approaches all-time highs
Capital Market Outlook June 15, 2026: sticky inflation and thin equity risk premium are vulnerabilities

decisions.invalidation: CPI re-accelerates, or Fed signals hawkish pivot, or S&P breaks below 200-day MA

summary: First session - hold all four existing positions; no trades. MSFT class action backward-looking, Q3 Azure +40% intact. NVDA chip price deflation watched; shareholder meeting June 24 is near-term catalyst. 91% gross long, $853 cash, no room or clear catalyst to add.
open_theses: MSFT: Azure +40% growth and AI ARR $37B justify holding through class action noise; Q4 FY26 late-July is thesis checkpoint
NVDA: Hold through June 24 shareholder meeting for Blackwell/Vera ramp update; monitor B200 rental prices weekly
QQQ: Ride AI capex supercycle via broad tech exposure; only long-signal name in portfolio
SPY: Defensive ballast; watch for macro regime change (CPI, Fed)
